title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
Jane Hobbs confesses to Hercule Poirot that she failed to participate in the planned "execution" at the Browns Hotel. Although she was part of the conspiracy involving Semple and others to frame Nancy Duane, Jane admits she was overwhelmed by fear and went to "The Happy Cafe" instead of meeting the group at six o'clock. She expresses fear that Richard Nigges would kill her for her failure to appear.

Poirot realizes that the identical positioning of the three murder victims—Harriet Sipprell, Ada Gransbury, and Richard Nigges—was a deliberate attempt by the conspirators to make multiple deaths appear to be the work of a single, obsessive killer. The bodies were arranged upright between tables and chairs to mimic a ritualistic execution. However, Poirot deduces that Richard Nigges actually committed suicide. To maintain the illusion of a single killer and protect the plot to frame Nancy, Nigges hid a key behind fireplace tiles to simulate an escape through a window. The cufflinks in Nigges' mouth were positioned differently than the others because he died alone from poison, causing his body to convulse and the cufflink to slip. Mrs. Vane suggests Nigges may have intended to end his life to escape guilt regardless of Jane's absence.

Poirot instructs Jane to stay home until he or Mr. Cattermole returns. Afterward, Stanley Bill informs Poirot that a doctor named Ambrose Flavard has gone to Scotland Yard to report that a woman named Margaret Ernst has been attacked and is dying. She has requested to see Mr. Cader before she passes.
